methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $1.5B - $4.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.6x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Ultima Genomics
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series C), post-money $4B

private.name: Insitro
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series C), post-money $2.4B

private.name: Colossal Biosciences
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.5B

data.narrative:
Tubulis, a Series C biotech company founded by Dominik Schumacher and Jonas Helma-Smets, has raised €436M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 11 comparable companies across Biotech & Health—3 public traded entities (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private firms including Ultima Genomics, Insitro, and Colossal Biosciences.

The peer-median method applies directly and yields $2.4B. This reflects the median valuation across three same-sector, same-stage private comparables: Ultima Genomics at $4.0B, Insitro at $2.4B, and Colossal Biosciences at $1.5B, establishing a range of $1.5B to $4.0B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples cannot be applied; the Biotech & Health public median stands at 6.6x across the three traded peers, but Tubulis does not disclose revenue, precluding this triangulation method.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $2.0B to $2.8B, centered on $2.4B. This range reflects the median peer valuation with modest adjustment bands. A single applicable method limits triangulation robustness. Key uncertainties include Tubulis's unreported revenue and profitability metrics, which would enable public-comp cross-validation, and the concentration of value signals in a narrow private peer set where stage and sector alignment, while tight, do not fully isolate for company-specific risk factors.
